claim_boundary: This is an empirical/numerical route audit. It does not prove global contraction, but tests whether the KKT fixed-point map has contraction behavior on the sampled domain.
fixed_point_map: T_i(x)=sqrt((24/w) P_x(i in B)), RMS-normalized; interior KKT points are fixed points.
interpretation: If the map were globally contractive in a suitable projective metric, fixed-point uniqueness would follow.
The audit measures log-coordinate derivative radii, singular norms, monotonicity of KKT iteration, and Hilbert projective contraction on sampled pairs.
This narrows the proof route to a possible projective contraction / KKT uniqueness theorem, or rejects it if expansion is observed.
